claim: Transient IFN-γ exposure leads to long-lived infammatory responses in cancer cells due to IFN-γ retention on the cell surface.

result.claim: Transient IFN-γ exposure leads to long-lived infammatory responses in cancer cells due to IFN-γ retention on the cell surface.
result.reasoning: The searches for similar propositions and chunks did not yield any direct evidence to support or refute the claim that "Transient IFN-γ exposure leads to long-lived inflammatory responses in cancer cells due to IFN-γ retention on the cell surface." While some results mentioned IFN-γ, inflammatory responses in cancer, or the presence of cytokines on cell surfaces, none of the retrieved information explicitly linked all components of the claim: transient exposure, long-lived responses, and IFN-γ retention on the cell surface as the underlying mechanism. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence in the knowledge base to verify this claim.

evidence.text: Mesenchymal stromal cells and interstitial stromal cells acquire podoplanin under interferon-γ stimuli.
evidence.source: Role of Podoplanin-Positive Cells in Cardiac Fibrosis and Angiogenesis After Ischemia.

evidence.text: Mitochondrial impairment in immune cells exacerbates inflammation in cancer.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial dysfunction acts as a modulator of the immunometabolic route for activating the cytosolic DNA sensor pathway in triggering innate immunosurveillance.

evidence.text: In all cell lines, PD-L1 and MHC class I proteins were upregulated following treatment with mouse recombinant interferon (IFN)-γ for 24 h.
evidence.source: MET pathway inhibition increases chemo-immunotherapy efficacy in small cell lung cancer.

evidence.text: Mast cells produce interferon-γ (IFN-γ).
evidence.source: Deficiency and pharmacological stabilization of mast cells reduce diet-induced obesity and diabetes in mice

evidence.text: Abrogation of endothelial Notch unleashed interferon gamma responses in the tumor microenvironment.
evidence.source: Endothelial cell Notch signaling programs cancer-associated fibroblasts to promote tumor immune evasion

evidence.text: In all cell lines, PD-L1 and MHC class I protein expression were upregulated following treatment with mouse recombinant interferon (IFN)-γ for 24 h.
evidence.source: MET pathway inhibition increases chemo-immunotherapy efficacy in small cell lung cancer.

evidence.text: Type I and II interferons (IFNs) are involved in mechanisms that regulate immune responses in cancer.
evidence.source: Role of Type I and II interferons in colorectal cancer and melanoma

evidence.text: TNF secretes interferon-gamma (IFN-γ) during the immune response.
evidence.source: Role of Tumor Necrosis Factor in Tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Macrophages become classically activated by exposure to the cytokine IFN-γ and the tumor necrosis factor (TNF).
evidence.source: The many faces of macrophage activation

evidence.text: H2-M3–restricted T cells produce interferon γ.
evidence.source: H2-M3–Restricted T Cells in Bacterial Infection

evidence.text: MLCK activity has been demonstrated to mediate intestinal epithelial barrier dysfunction induced by interferon γ (IFNγ).
evidence.source: Loss of guanylyl cyclase C (GCC) signaling leads to dysfunctional intestinal barrier.

evidence.text: SA increased pro-inflammatory cytokines in cancer cells
evidence.source: Inhibitory Effects of Syringic Acid on Endometrial Cancer Cell Growth and Migration and Its Synergistic Suppression with Doxorubicin.

evidence.text: Dox increased pro-inflammatory cytokines in cancer cells
evidence.source: Inhibitory Effects of Syringic Acid on Endometrial Cancer Cell Growth and Migration and Its Synergistic Suppression with Doxorubicin.

evidence.text: PM21-NK cells were stained intracellularly for IFN-γ.
evidence.source: Delivery of a novel membrane-anchored Fc chimera enhances NK cell-mediated killing of tumor cells and persistently virus-infected cells.

evidence.text: γ-irradiation can induce non-reparable DNA damage in cancer cells.
evidence.source: Spatiotemporal Mislocalization of Nuclear Membrane-Associated Proteins in γ-Irradiation-Induced Senescent Cells.

evidence.text: PM21-NK cells were stained intracellularly for IFN-γ.
evidence.source: Delivery of a novel membrane-anchored Fc chimera enhances NK cell-mediated killing of tumor cells and persistently virus-infected cells.

evidence.text: T cell–derived interferon-γ can directly target intestinal stem cells to induce their apoptosis.
evidence.source: T cell–derived interferon-γ programs stem cell death in immune-mediated intestinal damage

evidence.text: NK cells incubated with target cancer cells expressing NA-Fc4 had an increase in IFN-γ staining compared to control cells in the case of A549 cells.
evidence.source: Delivery of a novel membrane-anchored Fc chimera enhances NK cell-mediated killing of tumor cells and persistently virus-infected cells.

evidence.text: IFN-γ expression was quantified in tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) from B16F10 tumor-bearing CD244fl/fl and CD244fl/flLysMcre mice.
evidence.source: Targeted deletion of CD244 on monocytes promotes differentiation into anti-tumorigenic macrophages and potentiates PD-L1 blockade in melanoma.

evidence.text: LTB4 was detected in cells exposed to cytokine.
evidence.source: Aspirin modulates production of pro-inflammatory and pro-resolving mediators in endothelial cells.
